source_quote: On February 19, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to the Company to acquire us for a per share price of $19.25. The indication of interest was supported by a highly confident financing letter from Jefferies Finance LLC, contained a request for a forty-five day exclusivity period and contemplated a "go-shop" period following the signing of any definitive merger agreement.

additional_note: NMC indicated this was 'the highest price it was willing to offer'. Exclusivity period request was 45 days.

source_quote: In mid-June 2016, at the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF instructed all potential buyers to submit non-binding letters of intent (“LOIs”) by July 20, 2016, together with mark-ups of the forms of merger agreement, disclosure letter and voting agreement, which GHF indicated would be posted to the internet data site.

additional_note: Party C was not previously part of the process; approached and executed CA in early July 2016
comments_3: [info:date_inferred_from_rough] phrase: 'early July 2016'
source_quote: In early July 2016, a potential strategic buyer that had not previously been part of the process (“Party C”) approached the Company’s management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company. After executing a confidentiality agreement, Party C was provided the memorandum concerning the Company provided to the other potential buyers.

additional_note: IOI submitted after Party C joined the process late; Party C had management presentation by telephone on July 14, 2016
comments_2: Filing calls this an 'IOI' (indication of interest); per §G1 'non-binding indication of interest' is an informal trigger. Party C was a late joiner submitting before the LOI deadline.
comments_3: NA
source_quote: On July 12, 2016, Party C submitted an IOI with an offer price per share of $21.00 (implying an equity value of $108 million). Subsequently, Party C was provided access to the internet data site and the Company’s management held a management presentation with Party C by telephone on July 14, 2016.

additional_note: Bidder elected not to submit LOI by July 20, 2016 deadline; self-withdrew at informal stage

comments_3: NA
source_quote: G&W submitted an LOI on July 21, 2016 to acquire the Company for a price of $21.15 per share (subject to a three-week exclusive diligence period). The price included $20.02 cash at closing and $1.13 in the form of a contingent value right, the value of which was based on the subsequent sale of the Company’s South Quay property (“CVR”).

additional_note: LOI included mark-up of draft merger agreement and voting agreement
comments_2: Filing describes as 'non-binding letter of intent'; per §G1 'non-binding' is an informal trigger. LOI submitted in response to GHF's mid-June instruction to submit LOIs.

source_quote: On August 1, 2016, Party D submitted a revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share and Party E submitted a revised LOI, along with financing support, from Party F, at a price of $23.81 per share. Both revised proposals contained a 30-day due diligence period.

additional_note: Declined to shorten 30-day diligence period; Company declined to commit not to enter agreement with another bidder; Party D indicated would not proceed
comments_3: NA
source_quote: Party D declined to shorten its due diligence period and requested that the Company commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during its 30-day due diligence period. In light of the active interest of Party B and G&W, the Company declined to make such a commitment, at which point Party D indicated that it would not proceed with further due diligence at that time.

additional_note: Withdrew revised $23.81 proposal; confirmed original $21.26 proposal still stood; effectively withdrawn from active contention
comments_3: NA
source_quote: Party E withdrew its revised proposal on August 2, 2016 (but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share)

additional_note: Final LOI $25.00/share all-cash (no CVR); submitted with merger agreement and voting agreement markups; G&W completed physical diligence August 10-11
comments_2: Still described as a 'revised LOI' (non-binding) per §G1 informal trigger. Although submitted with merger agreement markups, the filing does not describe it as a binding bid or financing commitment. Board still needed to evaluate and negotiate final agreement.
comments_3: NA
source_quote: G&W submitted a revised LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $25.00 per share in cash, which excluded the previously proposed CVR, together with mark-ups of the merger agreement and the voting agreement. G&W’s LOI further indicated that the offer would expire at 6:00 p.m. on the following day.

additional_note: Party B declined to raise its $24.00/share offer in response to G&W's $25.00 bid
comments_3: NA
source_quote: Party B indicated that it would not increase its price.

additional_note: Merger agreement signed August 12, 2016; $25.00/share all-cash; Pullman Acquisition Sub Inc. is merger sub; Company, G&W, and Eder Trusts also executed voting agreement
comments_3: NA
source_quote: Shortly thereafter, Hinckley Allen and Simpson Thacher finalized the transaction documents and the Company and G&W executed the merger agreement, and the Company, G&W and the Eder Trusts executed the voting agreement.

source_quote: On August 27, 2013, BofA Merrill Lynch, as instructed by the Special Committee, sent a letter to each of Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona instructing such bidders to submit revised written proposals no later than September 9, 2013 if such bidders wished to proceed further in the process to pursue an in-depth evaluation of Mac-Gray with the objective of making a revised offer.

additional_note: Final proposal; all-cash $20.75/share

source_quote: On September 18, 2013, CSC/Pamplona submitted a proposal for an all-cash purchase price of $20.75 per share, reiterating that Pamplona was committed to provide 100% of the capital required to consummate the transaction and requesting that CSC/Pamplona and Mac-Gray agree to engage in exclusive negotiations for two weeks.

additional_note: Revised indication of interest; $19.00 cash + $2.50 in options = $21.50 total per share; options vest contingent on performance
comments_2: Informal per §G1: filed as 'revised indication of interest'; contingent options component renders this non-definitive; no firm financing commitment for cash component
comments_3: [info:contingent_type_ambiguous] Party B options are performance-vesting equity, not a CVR in the traditional sense; classified as 'cvr' (contingent value right) per §H2 default
source_quote: On September 18, 2013, Party B also submitted a revised indication of interest with a purchase price that Party B valued at $21.50 per share, including $19.00 of cash to be paid at closing and the remaining per share price to be paid in the form of options to be issued to Mac-Gray stockholders, representing 10% of the new equity with a strike price equal to Party B's initial cost basis.

additional_note: Party A reiterated prior IOI $18-19/share as 'best and final offer' via telephone
comments_2: Formal per §G1 'best and final offer' trigger; Party A explicitly stated this was their best and final offer in the final round

additional_note: Special Committee selected CSC/Pamplona for exclusivity; Party A's $18-19/share was below CSC/Pamplona's $20.75; Party A's 'best and final' offer not advanced

source_quote: The Special Committee concluded that the revised indication of interest submitted by CSC/Pamplona had the potential of delivering the most value to stockholders, provided greater certainty and involved less potential risk than the other indications. However, the Special Committee also concluded that, primarily in view of potential synergies between Mac-Gray and CSC, CSC/Pamplona should be in a position to increase their offer price above $20.75.

additional_note: Special Committee chose CSC/Pamplona over Party B; concerned about risks of Party B options and financing structure
comments_3: [soft:drop_agency_ambiguous] No explicit rejection statement for Party B; DropTarget inferred from Special Committee selecting CSC/Pamplona for exclusivity, implicitly not advancing Party B
source_quote: The Special Committee noted that the indication of interest submitted by Party B, including the Party B options, was on its face higher than the CSC/Pamplona proposal but further discussed the risks and complexities involved with measuring and achieving the performance required to vest the Party B options. The Special Committee also noted that Party B would likely be financing the transaction with a combination of equity from affiliated funds and third party debt capital, which would involve more risk than the Pamplona-funded CSC/Pamplona proposal, which did not require any third party financing.

additional_note: CSC/Pamplona confirmed $21.25/share 'last and best offer' via telephone; exclusivity agreement signed Sept 24
comments_2: Formal per §G1 'last and best' offer trigger; confirmed as last and best price in telephone call accepting exclusivity terms; no financing contingency

additional_note: Merger agreement executed; Pamplona commitment letter delivered; MacDonald and Moab voting agreements became effective
